title: The hour before the world wakes up
caption:
One hour. No one watching. No notifications. No performance.

The discipline that changes a man isn't the one he posts about — it's the one at 5 AM when the house is still dark and nobody knows he showed up.

Win the alarm first. The first battle of the day isn't your boss or your temptation — it's the snooze button. Phone off the nightstand. Alarm on the dresser. Remove the decision.

Feed the Word before the feed. Whoever you let in first sets the frame for everything after. "Your word is a lamp to my feet." — Psalm 119:105. Not a spotlight. A lamp. Enough for the next step.

Work while the world is asleep. An hour of focused work before the notifications start is worth three mid-afternoon. The man ahead before 8 AM moves differently than the man playing catch-up all day.

And on the hard mornings — the tired ones, the ones you don't want — those are the actual reps. That's the morning that forms you. Not the ones that come easy.

Character isn't built in the good mornings. It's built in the ones you don't want.

—

Save this for tomorrow morning.

#discipline #christianmen #faithoverfear #godlymen #manofgod #disciplineequalsfreedom #earlymorning #thework
